entries.title: Commentary on Lamentations on lamentations 3:38
entries.excerpt: "Is it not from the mouth of the Most High that good and evil come?" Namely from good results prosperity, from bad results adversity. For, in such an idea is excluded the error of those persons who claim everything happens from chance, like Cicero, the Roman philosopher. However, the apostle and evangelist John 1:3 asserts: "All things were made through him; and without him was not anything made that was made." And the prophet Isaiah 45:7 asserts the Lord God: "I form light and create darkness, I make weal and create woe, I am the Lord, who do all these things."
